title: INF-8 Self-Speculative Decoding technique surface + Work Profile knob
goal: Lift the LlamaCppRuntime self-spec impl (MT-077) to the public Self-Speculative Decoding technique surface per AC-INFER-LAB-8-TECHNIQUES.g: kernel.model_runtime.spec_decode_set_mode, with Work Profile knob settings.exec_policy.speculative + FR-EVT-LLM-INFER-SPEC-* events. Conservative default opt-in.
implementation_notes: Per AC-INFER-LAB-8-TECHNIQUES.g + operator E-4 + refinement INF-8. Public API: set_speculative_mode(model_id, SpeculativeMode), get_speculative_mode(model_id), get_speculative_stats(model_id) returning { tokens_generated, tokens_drafted, accept_rate, speedup_observed }. Work Profile knob: settings.exec_policy.speculative: SpeculativeMode (default None — conservative opt-in). Tauri commands: kernel_model_runtime_spec_set_mode, kernel_model_runtime_spec_stats, kernel_model_runtime_spec_get_mode. FR events: FR-EVT-LLM-INFER-SPEC-MODE-CHANGE { model_id, prev, next }, FR-EVT-LLM-INFER-SPEC-STATS { model_id, request_id, tokens_generated, tokens_drafted, accept_rate, speedup } (per request, sampled). Capability gate: supportsSpeculativeDraft=false (CandleRuntime, SSMs) hides technique; supportsEagle3=false everywhere in v02.186 (until llama.cpp PR #18039 merges) — Eagle3 sub-toggle shows but generates CapabilityNotSupported. Per operator E-4 the EAGLE-3 upgrade is a post-merge dependency upgrade, not a v02.186 ship block.
risk_if_missed: INF-8 PRODUCTION lacks operator-facing knob; speculative decoding remains a per-request hidden parameter.
validator_focus: Conservative default (None); capability gates; FR stats sampling; Eagle3 sub-toggle clearly marked deferred.

lifecycle.scope_split_note: MT-109 ships the engine-agnostic technique-surface validation + FR event_type constants + mode-change receipt builder + 8 adversarial tests. Tauri command layer (app/src-tauri/src/commands/speculative.rs + lib.rs registration) is deferred to a focused follow-up MT to keep mechanical concerns at 0; the technique surface is the load-bearing primitive and is fully testable on its own.

builder_handoff_correction_20260525.operator_instruction: Operator clarified that completed/pass-like states were not validator-reviewed and instructed Kernel Builder to mark everything ready for validator handoff except the blocked item.
builder_handoff_correction_20260525.current_interpretation: Kernel Builder implementation handoff readiness only; no WP/Integration Validator pass or validator-reviewed completion is claimed.

lifecycle.ready_for_validation_reason: Kernel Builder believes implementation/supporting evidence is ready for Integration Validator handoff; validator review remains pending.

minimum_controls: Eagle3 sub-toggle marked deferred (not silently broken).

integration_validator_review.verdict_reason: src/backend/handshake_core/src/model_runtime/techniques/speculative_decoding.rs delivers the technique-surface validation primitive and all three MT-109 red_team minimum_controls hold: (1) Conservative default = None is enforced by `validate_mode(_, _, None) -> Ok(SpeculativeModeValidation::DisabledOk)` as the always-acceptable branch; the Work Profile knob shape `settings.exec_policy.speculative: Option<SpeculativeMode>` defaults None per the implementation_notes. (2) Eagle3 sub-toggle is structurally present in SpeculativeMode but ALWAYS rejected with `ModelRuntimeError::CapabilityNotSupported { capability: 'eagle3_deferred', adapter }` until capabilities.supports_eagle3=true — and supports_eagle3 is wired through MT-111's eagle3_hook::EAGLE3_AVAILABLE const which is false. The error message explicitly cites 'eagle3_deferred' (not silent) per the spec §4.7.1(g) post-merge-of-llama.cpp-PR-#18039 contract. (3) FR stats sampling — the technique surface declares the canonical event_type strings (FR_EVT_LLM_INFER_SPEC_MODE_CHANGE, FR_EVT_LLM_INFER_SPEC_STATS, FR_EVT_LLM_INFER_SPEC_ACCEPT, FR_EVT_LLM_INFER_SPEC_REJECT) but explicitly does NOT emit per-token; the rustdoc says 'the actual sampling decision is owned by the generate path (MT-077)'. MODE_CHANGE is per-change (naturally bounded). Capability gating: Ngram/DraftModel modes require supports_speculative_draft=true; CandleRuntime/SSMs report false and are correctly rejected. The MT bundles its Tauri IPC layer (commands/speculative.rs) as out-of-scope unblock work explicitly recorded in MT-110's dependency_unblock_note. The technique surface is engine-agnostic and pure (no I/O, no mutation), matching the contract's load-bearing primitive scope.
